lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Oh, hello. I'd love to chat right now, but I'm a bit busy. Perhaps you could come back and chat another time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. Sorry to bother you!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Not at all! Farewell!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Hello there. I'm Rat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a what?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: No, no. My name is Rat. Rat Burgiss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ohhhhh, well, what's up, Ratty?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: It's Rat, thank you. And I, uh...heh...I seem to be in a bit of trouble here, as you can probably see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what seems to be the matter?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Well, I'm a trader by nature and I was on the way to Varrock with my cart here when I was set upon by outlaws! They ransacked my cart and stole some very important papers that I must get back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's awful! You're lucky to be alive!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, I know! I don't know how I survived.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you weren't worth killing?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Look, do you want to help me or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! Tell me what you need me to do.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Right, now I heard those outlaws say something about having a small camp site somewhere to the north-west of Varrock. They headed off to the north-west of here. They took 5 pages with them.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Kill the outlaws and get those papers back from them for me. Here's a folder in which you can put the pages. Be careful, though; those outlaws are tough.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: When you find all 5 pages, put them in the folder and bring them back to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, Ratty! I won't let you down!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Oh, wait a minute. I was going to give you a folder in which to collect my papers, but you haven't got enough room for it. Talk to me when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, I have to go!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Oh well. I'll just wait here then and maybe some nice person will help me. Perhaps...one day...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, are you guilt tripping me?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: No! Not at all! Nuh-uh! Nope! Hardly! Noooo, no no no!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Well, maybe a bit...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good luck with that!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's not what...oh, never mind!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The papers were the only valuable thing you had?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Uh...yes. I guess so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't sell anything worth having?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Ah! Uhh...no, not really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not much of a trader are you?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: No...I mean yes... Look, that's besides the point. Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall I get them back for you?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: You mean you want to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear. Well, I hope you get to Varrock okay!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Uh...so you can't help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry. I'm a bit busy right now.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Don't you want another quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I'm too busy right now.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Well, fine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good!!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: So go then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Go then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm gone!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Ah! You're back! Excellent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! Tell me what you need me to do.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Right, now I heard those outlaws say something about having a small camp site somewhere to the north-west of Varrock. They headed off to the north-west of here. They took 5 pages with them.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Kill the outlaws and get those papers back from them for me. Here's a folder in which you can put the pages. Be careful, though; those outlaws are tough.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: When you find all 5 pages, put them in the folder and bring them back to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, Ratty! I won't let you down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't remember what I am supposed to do!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Head north-west from here into the forest north-west of Varrock. Find the Outlaw Camp and kill the outlaws there.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Collect the pages of my document for me and put them into the folder I gave you. When the folder contains 5 pages, bring it to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the folder you gave me. Do you have another one?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Sure. Here you go. I'll add it to your account.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My account? Am I in debt?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: No, it's just the way you're standing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, that's the folder I gave you. I need you to fill it with pages for me.

sections.title: Using the folder and pages on Rat Burgiss

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes! That's one of my papers! Gather all 5 of them and put them into that folder I gave to you.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Ah! It looks like you have found some of the pages I need. You still need more, though.

sections.title: Using the pages on the folder
Handing the completed folder over to Rat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rat, I think I lost that letter you gave me!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: That's probably because you've replaced it with something else, by the looks of it! Talk to me when you have some space in your inventory!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Goodness me! Not much of a messenger, are you? Here's another one; try not to lose it this time! I've charged the parchment to your account.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you take a cheque?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: No thanks. I prefer tartan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Rat! I got your pages back!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Excellent! I knew you could help! Let me take those from you, there.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Now, I liked the way you handled yourself on that last little 'mission' I gave you there, so I'm going to let you in on a little secret!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! Wait! Let me guess! You're actually a rich prince in disguise who wants to help poor people like me!?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Uhhh...no. No, that's not it. You know, on second thought, I think I'll keep my secret for now. Look, instead, you can do another job for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All work and no play makes [player name] a dull adventurer!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, well, I'm sure that may be the case. However, what I want you to do is take this letter to someone for me. It's in a different language so, trust me, you won't be able to read it.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Take it to a wizard named Surok Magis who resides in the Varrock Place Library. I'll see about some sort of reward for your work when I get myself sorted out here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Letter. Wizard. Varrock. Library. Got it!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, good luck then.

sections.title: Using the wand or diary on Surok Magis

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Yes, I just gave that to you. Don't break it already!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: No thanks, I don't need that.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Ah! You're back. Have you found the things I need yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me what I am doing again?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Take this metal wand. You will also need 15 chaos runes. When you get to the Chaos Altar, use the wand on the altar itself. This should infuse the runes into the wand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the wand!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Somehow, I knew that would happen so I have made a few spares for just such an occasion!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Here you g...hang on! There's one still in your inventory! I just saw it! You don't need two and they're not worth anything...yet! Now get going already; there's not much time!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You would lie to an arch-wizard? There is one in your bank! I can sense it! Go and retrieve that one first!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You have no space for another one! Lose some of that useless junk in your inventory first!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Here you are. Try not to lose this one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much gold will I get?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hmmmm. That all depends on how quickly you get me that infused wand! Work fast and well for me, [player name], and I will make you rich, famous and powerful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Powerful, eh? How powerful?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: That depends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Depends on what?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: On how soon you stop yapping and get me that wand! Now get moving!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I ask about the diary?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Of course. What would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is this Sin'keth?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Sin'keth Magis? From what I have read, it would appear he was a great leader of the Dagon'hai Mages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Dagon-what?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hmph! If you read the diary or perused that tome I mentioned, you would know who they are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, wait! Isn't your last name Magis too? Are you related to him?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: No. Not at all. Purely coincidence! Now, haven't you got work to do already?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did it come from?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: How I came to have the diary is of no concern to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oooo, tetchy!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I suggest that you concentrate on getting the things I need and stop asking pointless questions!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the safe route?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I could tell you, of course; I know more or less exactly where it is. However, that would take all the fun out of this, wouldn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, really! It's okay! You can tell me where it is!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: No, I think you should see if you can find it for yourself. I have given you the diary and, along with the tome around here, you should be able to find that alternate route easily enough on your own!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have another one?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You want another copy of the diary?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I seem to have misplaced the last one.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hang on! I can see the last copy I gave you in your inventory there! You don't need another one!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Fine, but this diary is important you know! It has secrets that you should not share around with just anybody!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I forgot the question.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Fair enough. Go then. I expect you have a lot of work to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is that tome you mentioned, again?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Like I said, it's lying around here in the library somewhere. It should be easy enough to find.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hah! Come for my Aphro-Dizzy-Yak spell! Want someone to fall madly in love with you, eh? Not surprised with a face like that, to be honest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't come here to be insulted!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Really? Well, with ears like that, you do surprise me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, look. I have a letter for you.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Really? Well then, let me see it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. I had it here a moment ago. Where is it?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hah! You've lost it already? You can't get good help these days!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm very sorry! Let me go get another one!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: As you must! Now be off with you! I'm busy studying here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here it is!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Of all the luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you destroy the letter?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: None of your business! It's a secret!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, there seems to be a lot of them going around at the moment.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Of course. Hmmm. However, I could let you in on another secret, if you like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on, then!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: My secret is this. I have been spending time here in the palace library trying to discover some ancient spells and magics.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: In my research, I have uncovered a most astounding spell that will allow me to transform simple clay into solid gold bars!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, what do you need?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I will only need a couple of items. The first is very simple: an ordinary bowl to use as a casting vessel.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You should be able to find one of these at any local store here in Varrock. I would go myself but I am...uh...busy with my research.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: The other item is much harder. I need a metal wand infused with chaos magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I get something like that?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Take this metal wand. You will also need 15 chaos runes. When you get to the Chaos Altar, use the wand on the altar itself. This should infuse the runes into the wand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How on earth do you know about Runecrafting? I thought only a few people knew of it.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hah! Don't presume to think that those wizards in their fancy tower are the only people to have heard of Runecrafting! Now pay attention!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You will need to have the 15 chaos runes in your inventory. Make sure you also have either a chaos talisman or chaos tiara to complete the infusion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get a talisman or a tiara?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't know. You will need to search for one.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Bring the infused wand and a bowl back to me and I will make us both rich!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: One more thing. I have uncovered information here in the library which may be of use to you. It tells of a safe route to the Chaos Altar that avoids the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What is it?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: It is an old tome...a history book of sorts. It's somewhere here in the library. I forget where I left it, but it should be easy enough for you to find.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I have also given you a copy of a diary I...uh...acquired. It may also help you to find that which you seek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it a BIG secret?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: It's a very big secret!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bigger than a house?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Er, yes! It's...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bigger than a volcano??

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Yes. Look, it's big, alright? Now listen and I'll tell you.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: My secret is this. I have been spending time here in the palace library trying to discover some ancient spells and magics.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: In my research, I have uncovered a most astounding spell that will allow me to transform simple clay into solid gold bars!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Now I am ready to use the spell to create all the gold I...uh...the city wants. I would gladly share this gold with you; I simply need a few more things!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, what do you need?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I will only need a couple of items. The first is very simple: an ordinary bowl to use as a casting vessel.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You should be able to find one of these at any local store here in Varrock. I would go myself but I am...uh...busy with my research.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: The other item is much harder. I need a metal wand infused with chaos magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I get something like that?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Take this metal wand. You will also need 15 chaos runes. When you get to the Chaos Altar, use the wand on the altar itself. This should infuse the runes into the wand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How on earth do you know about Runecrafting? I thought only a few people knew of it.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hah! Don't presume to think that those wizards in their fancy tower are the only people to have heard of Runecrafting! Now pay attention!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You will need to have the 15 chaos runes in your inventory. Make sure you also have either a chaos talisman or chaos tiara to complete the infusion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get a talisman or a tiara?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't know. You will need to search for one.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Bring the infused wand and a bowl back to me and I will make us both rich!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: One more thing. I have uncovered information here in the library which may be of use to you. It tells of a safe route to the Chaos Altar that avoids the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What is it?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: It is an old tome...a history book of sorts. It's somewhere here in the library. I forget where I left it, but it should be easy enough for you to find.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I have also given you a copy of a diary I...uh...acquired. It may also help you to find that which you seek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I have other things to do for now!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Very well, then. I hope these other matters of 'great' importance outweigh the need to make a LOT of gold. A WHOLE lot of gold. Gold. Yes, gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, why are you repeating yourself like that?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Dramatic effect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

sections.title: Talking to Rat Burgiss after handing the letter to Surok Magis

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Ah, [player name]! Good to see you! I heard you got my letter to Surok. Well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right! I did your leg work. Now, how about a reward?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, of course! I'd be happy to give you a reward, but I have a lot on my mind with...you know...trader...stuff!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed!

sections.title: Attempting to dig the statue of Saradomin before talking to Anna Jones

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: You're going to need a bronze pickaxe to get through there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A bronze one? Why bronze? They're pretty cheap and tacky.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Well, I don't claim to have a lot of knowledge about the ways of magic, but there's a strange protection spell on there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A protection spell?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Yes. Apparently the makers of this tunnel protected it against all forms of pickaxes except bronze.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Why bronze?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I don't think they thought anyone would actually try and use a bronze pickaxe as it's such a poor instrument!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well, thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Any time! Good luck!

sections.title: Talking to Anna Jones before digging the statue of Saradomin
Excavating the statue after talking to Anna Jones

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Be careful! You're going to bring the statue down on both of our heads if you go swinging wildly away like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what should I do?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I would suggest you improve your Mining skills a bit before you attempt this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'd better go and train some more then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: You did it! Oh, well done! How exciting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, well, I better see what's down there, then.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: You opened the tunnel; well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you going to go inside?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I'd better not. My orders were to open up the entrance and wait for Surok to arrive. When he gets here, we'll go in together. He promised me a lot of gold for this job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, then. I'm going in to have a look!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Ah. You must be [player name], right? I have a bronze pickaxe here for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks very much!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: My employer, Surok Magis, sent word to me that you may come to use the tunnel. You will need something to help you get in there. The pickaxe I've given to you should help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Okay, then. The tunnel awaits...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, but I already have one.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Oh. Alright then! My employer, Surok Magis, sent word to me that you may come to use the tunnel. You'll need your bronze pickaxe to get in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Okay, then. The tunnel awaits...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What tunnel?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Why, the Chaos Tunnel of course! I imagine Surok will have told you of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see. Well, what can you tell me about it?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Well, story and rumour has it that the Dagon'hai built a tunnel here under the statue of Saradomin that would allow them to visit the Chaos Altar without having to go through the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's extremely handy!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Yes, it is. Or, at least, it would be. Unfortunately, I haven't been able to get in there so far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: The tunnel entrance needs to be cleared before anyone can get in there. However, my Mining skill isn't high enough at all to do it myself. Perhaps you could have a go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, who are you?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: My name is Louisiana Jones, although most people call me Anna. I'm an archaeologist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Likewise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who does this statue represent?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: That, my dear, is the statue of the great god Saradomin himself. Stand and admire in awe for you are in the presence of greatness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Consider me awed!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Well story and rumour has it that the Dagon'hai built a tunnel under this statue of Saradomin that would allow them to visit the Chaos Altar without having to go through the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's extremely handy!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Yes, it is. Or, at least, it would be. I came to excavate the tunnel but, unfortunately, there so far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: The tunnel entrance needs to be cleared before anyone can get in there. However, my Mining skill just isn't what it used to be. Perhaps you could have a go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe. I might do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Okay, then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who does this statue represent?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

sections.title: Imbuing the wand at the Chaos Altar
Talking to Rat Burgiss with the infused wand/diary

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: What on earth is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't tell you. It's a secret!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I see! It looks pretty magical; I'd be careful with that if I were you!

sections.title: Talking to Surok after imbuing the wand

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made the wand that you asked me for, but I seem to have lost it!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You lost it? Those wands are extremely powerful and dangerous in the wrong hands! You better hope someone doesn't find it!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Hmph! In the meantime, here's another metal wand. Go and make another infused wand for me...and be more careful this time! My patience is growing thin!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You say you made the infused wand for me? Then why do you still have that metal wand I gave you? Go and do what I ask before I lose my temper and turn you into a worm or something!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the things you wanted!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: No, it looks like you still need to find me a bowl first. An ordinary bowl. You can get one from one of the stores around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the things you wanted!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Excellent! Well done! I knew that you would not let me down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...about this gold that you're going to give me?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: All in good time. I must prepare the spell first, and that will take a little time. While I am doing that, please take this letter to Rat, the trader outside the city who sent you here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, but I'll be back for my gold.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Yes, yes, yes. Now off you go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I ask about the diary?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Of course. What would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I forgot the question.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Fair enough. Go then. I expect you have a lot of work to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost that letter you gave me!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Goodness me! You do lose a lot of things! It's a wonder you can make it out of bed in the mornings! Well, here's another one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: It's not surprising with all that junk in your inventory! Get some room, first, then speak to me again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That letter was treasonous so I destroyed it!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Really? You destroyed it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You want another one, don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah...uh...yes...yes, I do.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Fine. Here you are. And stop all this complaining; it's getting me down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you really?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: That is not your concern! I am Surok Magis and I am more powerful than you can possibly imagine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's in it for me?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You get to live. For now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not that I'm complaining about the living part, but I was expecting something of more...er...value?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Ah, yes. The gold. Don't you worry, I am still working on that. Just deliver my letter for me, first. Now be off!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I will deliver the letter, but I will be back soon.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I am sure you will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I still get the gold?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: If that is your only concern, then yes, you do. But I must warn you, my patience with you is wearing thin. So go now and do my bidding!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I will deliver the letter, but I will be back soon.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I am sure you will.

sections.title: Talking to King Roald with Surok's letter in their inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your majesty, I think that you should see this letter.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Letter? Let me see. Where's Postie Pete? He usually deals with the mail around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This letter was delivered to me by hand, your majesty. I think you may be in some danger.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Hmmm. I see. I appreciate your concern. However, I assure you, I am quite safe here. My guards are on full alert at all times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think you understand...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I understand perfectly. Now, for all I know, that letter could be a fake ruse. We get a lot of that sort of thing and it causes a lot of hassle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to save your life!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: And you are shouting at your king! People who yell at their monarchs often find their heads become...loose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just saying that the letter is real.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Then why do you have it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because I'm supposed to deliv...oh.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: It seems to me that if that letter were genuine, I would be forced to arrest its bearer for treason. You understand.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: So what do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I...er...I reckon it's a fake! ...I guess...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Just as I thought! Now off you go!

sections.title: Giving the letter to Rat Burgiss

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I doing now?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: You need to go and see Zaff. He can be found at 'Zaffs Staffs', the staff shop in Varrock. He will tell you what to do next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think we can stop Surok?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Surok is a powerful mage and ruthless too. Who knows what will happen. I am sure that we will prevail, though. Don't worry, [player name]! You'll be fine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have anything to trade yet?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: No, I'm afraid I have nothing in stock, largely due to the fact that I'm not really a trader, as I just mentioned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I remember!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, your cart IS kind of small!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: What are you implying?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, nothing! It's very...er...cute!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Why exactly are you still here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I better go!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Okay, good luck!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Ah, [player name]! You've returned!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! I have a letter for you.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: This letter is treasonous! This does indeed confirm my worst fears. It is time I let you into my secret and hopefully this will answer any questions you may have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Go on.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I am not really a trader. I am the Commander of the Varrock Palace Secret Guard. VPSG for short.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I had a feeling you weren't a real trader due to the fact that you had nothing to sell! So why the secrecy?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I'm just getting to that. A short while ago, we received word that Surok had discovered a powerful mind-control spell and intended to use it on King Roald himself!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: He could control the whole kingdom that way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I can believe that. Surok's not the nicest person in Misthalin!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, but until now, the spell has been useless to him as he is currently under guard at the palace and not allowed to leave. He could not get the tools for the spell because if he left the palace he would be arrested!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh! I think I may have helped him by mistake, here. He promised me a big reward if I collected some items for him...but he said it was for a spell to make gold!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, we heard that somehow Surok had obtained the things he needed but we were not sure how. I thought it might have been you.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: However, I assumed you did not know of his plans; that is why you weren't arrested!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you! How can I help fix this mistake?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Okay, here's what I need you to do. One of my contacts has devised a spell that he is sure will be able to counteract the effects of the mind-control spell. I need you to visit him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, who is it?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: His name is Zaff. He runs a staff shop in Varrock. Go and speak to him and he will tell you what you should do. I will send word to him to let him know that you are coming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, sir! I'm on my way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and you have some explaining to do!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Why? Whatever do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This letter from Surok to you implies that you're involved in a treasonous plot! I should tell the guards about you!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I'm sure I don't know what you mean. Let me see the letter, please.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: This letter is treasonous! This does indeed confirm my worst fears. It is time I let you into my secret and hopefully this will answer any questions you may have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Go on.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I am not really a trader. I am the Commander of the Varrock Palace Secret Guard. VPSG for short.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I had a feeling you weren't a real trader due to the fact that you had nothing to sell! So why the secrecy?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I'm just getting to that. A short while ago, we received word that Surok had discovered a powerful mind-control spell and intended to use it on King Roald himself!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: He could control the whole kingdom that way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I can believe that. Surok's not the nicest person in Misthalin!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, but until now, the spell has been useless to him as he is currently under guard at the palace and not allowed to leave. He could not get the tools for the spell because if he left the palace he would be arrested!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh! I think I may have helped him by mistake, here. He promised me a big reward if I collected some items for him...but he said it was for a spell to make gold!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, we heard that somehow Surok had obtained the things he needed but we were not sure how. I thought it might have been you.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: However, I assumed you did not know of his plans; that is why you weren't arrested!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you! How can I help fix this mistake?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Okay, here's what I need you to do. One of my contacts has devised a spell that he is sure will be able to counteract the effects of the mind-control spell. I need you to visit him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, who is it?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: His name is Zaff. He runs a staff shop in Varrock. Go and speak to him and he will tell you what you should do. I will send word to him to let him know that you are coming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, sir! I'm on my way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, you're still here?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes. I still haven't found someone to help me yet, but I hear you've been very busy yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have? Who told you that?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Oh, I have my sources. Was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been told of your foul plans, Surok! You lied to me about the gold, so I've come to stop you!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Oh really? And what makes you think you have even the slightest chance of doing that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll fight you if I have to!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Enough! You have been of use to me before but now you are wasting my time! Be gone!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: What are you doing, [player name]? I'm right here! Why are you trying to summon me again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I was just testing the ring.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Well, stop playing around; I'm a busy man!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Okay, Surok, your time is ov...hey! What gives? You don't need me yet! Why did you use the ring?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I was just testing the ring.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Look, stop messing around! My shop doesn't look after itself, you know! Hmm. Perhaps I could invent a spell that would do that...

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Is that supposed to amuse me?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: No thanks, I don't need that.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Would you like to buy or sell some staffs? Or is there something else you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to ask you something else.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Go ahead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I doing again?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Make sure you have read the instructions I gave you about the ring. Then, when you are ready, go to Surok and arrest him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have another ring?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: You have one already. It's there in your inventory!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Yes, I have another one, but you don't have any space for it. Speak to me when your inventory is less full!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Well, I do happen to have another one here that you can have. Please try and be a bit more careful with this one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have the instructions again?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: You have one already. It's there in your inventory!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Yes, I have another one, but you don't have any space for it. Speak to me when your inventory is less full!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Well, I do happen to have another one here that you can have. Please try and be a bit more careful with this one!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Would you like to buy or sell some staffs? Or is there something else you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rat Burgiss sent me!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Ah, yes; You must be [player name]! Rat sent word that you would be coming. Everything is prepared. I have created a spell that will remove the mind control from the king.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so what's the plan?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Listen carefully. For the spell to succeed, the king must be made very weak. If his mind is controlled, you will need to fight him until he is all but dead.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Then and ONLY then, use your ring to summon me. I will teleport to you and cast the spell that will cure the king.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why must I summon you? Can't you come with me?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: I cannot. I must look after my shop here and I have lots to do. Rest assured, I will come when you summon me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so what do I do now?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: First, free up 2 inventory spaces so I can give you a couple of things.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Take this beacon ring and some instructions.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Once you have read the instructions, it will be time for you to arrest Surok.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Won't he be disinclined to acquiesce to that request?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Won't he what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Won't he refuse?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: I very much expect so. It may turn nasty, so be on your guard. I hope we can stop him before he can cast his spell! Make sure you have that ring I gave you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks, Zaff!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Rat has told me that you are to be made an honorary member of the VPSG so that you can arrest Surok. If you have any questions about this, ask Rat.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: One last thing: you must remember that as part of the VPSG, we must remain secretive at all times. For this reason, I cannot discuss matters such as this unless absolutely necessary!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! Thanks again!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Ooh! Shiny! I would take it, but I'd probably have to sell it. I have a wife and 17 children to feed!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Besides, you probably need it more than me right now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been to see Zaff and he has told me to go and arrest Surok.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Excellent! In that case, we are all set and ready to foil Surok's plans!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happens now?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Now it is all down to you, [player name]. As you have proven yourself in the tasks I have set you, I am making you an honorary member of the VPSG!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oooh! Do we have adventurer's insurance?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Well...no. But we do have a nice lady called Emma who makes the tea on Mondays.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well that sounds just as good!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Right then. You have an important task set out for you. Good luck, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are my orders again?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: You will need to go and speak to Surok now. Arrest him under the authority of the Varrock Palace Secret Guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right then.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: It may be tough and Surok will most likely not be arrested without a fight, so be on your guard!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me of the Dagon'hai?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: How do you know of them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surok gave me a diary that spoke of them. Do you know anything about them?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I see. The Dagon'hai were a powerful order of Zamorakian mages who resided here in this very city.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: However, the priests of Saradomin warred ferociously with them and tried to destroy them. The Dagon'hai, though, were very clever and extremely cunning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: They hid themselves away within the city and, rumour has it, they even had a way of getting to the Wilderness and back without ever being seen, although I have no idea how that might have been.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where are they now?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Nobody knows. They disappeared from the city a hundred years ago and very few people even remember who they were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do you know about them?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: My grandfather's brother, my great uncle, was a priest of Saradomin and he would tell me stories of the wars with the Dagon'hai as a child.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that ring I had!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Then you will have to go and speak to Zaff at Zaff's Staffs to get another one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At ease, soldier!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: You shouldn't mock your commanding officer, private! Don't forget, I can still have you arrested for conspiracy with Surok!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, yes, sorry! I forgot about that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are my orders again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What benefits do I get in the VPSG?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Unfortunately, as an honorary member of the VPSG, you only get to act under our authority. You aren't entitled to the full Secret Guard package.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I don't get the secret spy-pack then?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I'm afraid not. No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I need to go!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, you have an important job! Good luck, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surok!! Your plans have been uncovered! You are hereby under arrest on the authority of the Varrock Palace Secret Guard!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: So! You're with the Secret Guard, eh? I should have known! I knew you had ugly ears from the start...and your nose is too short!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give yourself up, Surok!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Never! I am Surok Magis, descendant of the High Elder Sin'keth Magis, rightful heir of the Dagon'hai Order! I will have my revenge on those who destroyed my people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The place is surrounded. There is nowhere to run!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Do you really wish to die so readily? Are you prepared to face your death?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bring it on!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: I am a Dagon'hai! I run from nothing. My spell has been completed and it is time for you to meet your end, [player name]! The king is now under my control!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Annach Narh Hin Dei!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: What's going on...?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I... must... kill... [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh! King Roald looks evil!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine! You win this time!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Get out of my sight then!

sections.title: Talking to Zaff if the player operates the ring too soon

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Would you like to buy or sell some staffs? Or is there something else you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you teleport us? We were winning!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: The king was not weak enough and Surok's power over him was still too strong! You must weaken the king further before summoning me. Otherwise, we will fail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I do now?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: I am afraid we must try again. We will defeat Surok this time. I am sure of it!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I will smite you, cur!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Have at you!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Take that, knave!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: A member of the Phoenix Gang!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Your gang isn't here now!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: That garden was awful!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I'm allergic to roses!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I hate gardens!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Back, minion of evil!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You're with the Vampyres!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: The king's still too strong! We had better get out of here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! Let me keep fighting!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: It's too late! I cast the spell as soon as you summoned me, but it was too early! We need to withdraw and plan our attack again!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Sin danna nim borha!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: The king's mind has been restored to him and he has been teleported away to safety. Now, to deal with Surok!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: No! All is lost! I must escape!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: You will not escape justice this time, Surok!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Mirra din namus!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Stop!!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Your teleport spell has been corrupted, Surok! I have placed a magic block on this room. You will remain here, under guard, in the library from now on.

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: No! My plans have been ruined! I was so close to success!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Thank you for your help, [player name]. I will put the room back in order and then I must leave. Surok is defeated and will be no more trouble for us. We will guard him more closely from now on!

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: Is that supposed to amuse me?

lines.speaker: Surok Magis
lines.text: You have foiled my plans, [player name]... I obviously underestimated you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Let this be a lesson to you.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Would you like to buy or sell some staffs? Or is there something else you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We did it! We beat Surok!

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Yes. You have done well, [player name]. You are to be commended for your actions!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was all in the call of duty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will happen with Surok now?

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Well, when I disrupted Surok's spell, he will have been sealed in the library, but we will still need to keep an eye on him, just in case.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: When you are ready, report back to Rat and he will reward you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I will.

lines.speaker: Zaff
lines.text: Sadly, however, after this I will not be able to discuss these matters with you again. They are secret and we must be vigilant. The VPSG will prevail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. I understand. Goodbye!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Well, [player name], how did it go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should have been there! There was this...and Surok was like...and I was...and then the King...and, and...uh...ahem! The mission was accomplished and the king has been saved.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: I take it that it went alright, then? That's great news!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Zaff has already briefed me on the events. We will arrange for Surok to be fed and watched. I think he will not be a problem any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, one thing bothers me. He's now stuck in the library, but wasn't that the reason we were in this mess in the first place?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Yes, you are right. But rest assured, we will be watching him much more closely from now on.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: You've done very well and have been a credit to the VPSG; perhaps one day there may be a place for you here!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: In the meantime, let me reward you for what you've done, give you a little training. I will be sure to call on you if we ever need help in the future.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, Rat!

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: Ah! [player name]! You did a fine service for us. You might make a good member of the VPSG one day, with a little training and a bit more muscle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, do you have any more jobs for me to do?

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: At the moment, no. Things seem pretty quiet. However, I have heard a rumour about something strange going on in...hmm, no, I think we can handle this one for now.

lines.speaker: Rat Burgiss
lines.text: But, who knows? We may need your assistance again soon. Thank you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any time, Rat!
